query: Bilt.me - Figma complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: 10 Best Appy Pie Alternatives for Building Real Native Apps | Bilt Blog | Bilt
results.content:
Bravo Studio is a dedicated Figma-to-native-app tool if design fidelity is your top priority.

Compared to Thunkable, which made AI-first project generation its headline 2026 feature, FlutterFlow treats AI as a supplement to its visual editor rather than the primary entry point.

Limitations to consider:

 Zapier explicitly notes it is not for the faint of heart as a standalone tool for non-technical users
 User sentiment is split: G2 rates it 4.5/5, but Trustpilot sits at 2.5/5 with recurring complaints about support and bugs in complex scenarios
 Store publishing is semi-manual on Standard+ plans: one-click builds are available, but metadata, screenshots, and compliance are still the user's responsibility [...] Limitations worth knowing before you commit:
 Published apps require an active paid subscription to stay live
 Backend extensibility is limited compared to Buildfire (150+ plugins) or FlutterFlow (Firebase, Supabase, Stripe, custom Dart)
 Recurring complaints on Capterra cite pricing changes that frustrated free-tier users who built projects under earlier terms

Pricing: Free (2k tokens, 3 public projects), Accelerator $19/mo (20k tokens), Builder $59/mo, Advanced $189/mo (100k+ tokens). Annual billing reduces costs across all tiers.

If your app is live and earning revenue, Thunkable's subscription dependency is a real risk. Bilt generates code you own outright, so your app keeps running regardless. [...] For a deeper look at prompt-led tools, see our best AI app builder comparison.

### 1. Bilt.me ✨

Bilt.me conversational app builder interface

Bilt.me conversational app builder interface

Describe the app you want to build. Bilt turns that idea into a real native app for iOS and Android, then helps with the signing, build, and App Store steps.

You do not need to code, install Xcode, figure out provisioning profiles, or hire a mobile developer just to get started.

Bilt is built for non-technical builders, founders, and vibecoders who want to ship the app, not spend weeks learning mobile tooling.

results.title: No response from the customer support for a week. | Figma Forum
results.content:
Hi ​@Major Developments - thanks for posting about this.

I can understand your frustration, especially when it comes to billing information. I found the tickets you submitted and flagged those to our team, so you should hear back soon regarding access to previous bills. In the meantime, let me know if you have any other questions.

Thanks for your patience, and have a great day!

Major Developments

Hi,

Thank you for your message. I do not have any further questions at this stage.

However, I am concerned about the lack of response, as it has been over a week without any updates or even an acknowledgment that our request has been received and is being processed. [...] Figma Inc Logo
Figma Inc Logo

Everything announced at Config 2026 🎉

Tom Reem
Figmate

# No response from the customer support for a week.

Major Developments

What kind of customer service is this?   
We were using Figma for the corporate for a long time. Once we cancel the subscription, we lost all this information and access to previous bills.   
We submitted a request, but they were no feedback for a week what so ever. This is so unprofessional. No options to contact an operator! Left to speak with a useless bot.

Worst experience ever.

## 4 replies

adamsmasher
Figmate

Hi ​@Major Developments - thanks for posting about this. [...] We are currently under time pressure from our finance team, as they need to close the year, and at this point, I do not have any information to share with them. We require three invoices, but so far nothing has been provided.

Gayani_S
Figmate

Hey ​@Major Developments, I’m stepping in on behalf of ​@adamsmasher to help here!

I’m really sorry for the lack of updates, especially given the time pressure you’re under with your finance team.

I can see the support team is already in the process of gathering the three invoices. I’ve flagged this internally as urgent and asked the team to prioritise it so this gets resolved as quickly as possible.

We’re actively keeping an eye on your ticket to make sure this is followed through. You should receive the invoices very soon.

results.title: Bilt Technologies, Inc. | BBB Complaints | Better Business Bureau
results.content:
back to help book the flight. Asking for help via their chat results in nothing, and they give scripted responses with no help. They have no phone support, and they state these specialists only respond via email but give zero timeframe and refuse to, leaving the customer hanging in the balance and suffering. I'm so frustrated, when this is something that should be resolved so simply it occured all within their own system. Meanwhile, they boast about how great of a company they are, yet how they treat their customers is not timely, helpful, or reliable. #### Customer Answer [...] Resolved:The complainant verified the issue was resolved to their satisfaction. Unresolved:The business responded to the dispute but failed to make a good faith effort to resolve it. Answered:The business addressed the issues within the complaint, but the consumer either a) did not accept the response, OR b) did not notify BBB as to their satisfaction. Unanswered:The business failed to respond to the dispute. Unpursuable:BBB is unable to locate the business.    I had the Bilt 1.0 card. In February, they transitioned to a different bank to the 2.0 changing some of the features of the card. Apparently, you can still pay rent on the card, but now it pulls from your checking account instead. It has been a very confusing process and Bilt has changed its features numerous times with the new [...] Date:05/07/2026

With the launch of the Bilt 2.0, housing payments are no longer charged against the card's credit line. Instead, they are automatically funded from the customer's linked bank account within one business day. This change was communicated in our updated terms, website, app, and card offer materials. Bile acknowledges the transition was confusing for some members and that Bilt support team at times provided inconsistent guidance that compounded her frustration. We sincerely apologize for that.

results.title: 10 Best Blink Alternatives for Non-Technical Builders in 2026 | Bilt Blog | Bilt
results.content:
User sentiment is the biggest warning sign.

 Trustpilot rating: 2.3/5 across 511 reviews
 Reported issues include slow support, glitches, billing frustration, and credits burning on bug fixes
 Teams that want steadier support may feel safer with tools that have stronger public feedback, such as Rork or Dreamflow

Native publishing is the second gap.

Base44 can prepare apps for store submission and generate IPA or AAB files, but the mobile output is still a lightweight native wrapper around the web app, not full native app generation.

Scalability is the third concern.

Base44 looks strongest at MVP stage. Trustpilot reviews back this up — at 2.3/5 from 511 users, scalability and support are the most common complaints past the MVP stage.

#### Why choose it over Blink [...] 1. Bilt.me - best for native iOS and Android app creation via AI conversation
2. Rork - AI chat platform for rapid native iOS prototypes with one-click App Store submission
3. Vibecode - prompt-based full-stack web and React Native app builder with conversational iteration
4. Base44 - no-code full-stack web app builder for indie developers and MVPs
5. a0 - YC-backed AI agent for production-ready React Native apps with built-in payments and analytics
6. RapidNative - mobile-first React Native builder from prompts, sketches, or Figma
7. Dreamflow - Flutter-based visual and AI hybrid app builder from the FlutterFlow team
8. Rocket - single-prompt full-stack generator with market research and competitor monitoring [...] | Buyer type | Best pick | Why |
 --- 
| Beginner | Apsy | Guided setup and free build-and-test flow |
| Solo founder | Bilt.me or Rork | Faster path to a real app, depending on publishing depth needed |
| Agency | RapidNative | Multiplayer editing and stronger collaboration |
| Enterprise team | Rocket | Shared workspaces, compliance controls, and broader team setup |

## Why teams switch to Bilt.me after trying the rest

Most builders hit the same wall. The app looks done at 80%, then the backend needs wiring, App Store compliance shows up, and the project stalls.

Bilt is built for that last mile. It handles native builds, code signing, and store submission so you are not stuck Googling provisioning profiles at midnight.

Bilt covers the parts that usually break the workflow:

results.title: Bilt to Spill? Customers Lash Out as Bilt’s Regulator Goes Missing
results.content:
Poor customer service delivered by AI chatbots. Bilt uses AI agents to deliver first-line customer service for customers with inquiries or issues related to Bilt’s products. However, borrowers’ testimonials describe Bilt’s AI agents as being unable to process basic requests for assistance and providing no viable path to receive support from a human representative. As FBW described, “users seeking to connect with a human support agent are complaining of multi-day wait times or no response at all.” [...] > “I submitted a rent payment through Bilt 2.0 - obsidian on February 27th. The money was withdrawn from my bank account, but my landlord still hasn’t received it. It was never clearly explained that adding a new payment card could require up to five business days or that a rental agreement upload would be required. When I tried to upload the document, the system repeatedly returned an error. The biggest issue: there is no real customer support. No phone number. No way to speak to a person. The AI chatbot is useless and only provides scripted responses. I am currently traveling and this delay has made my rent late, yet there is no urgency and no clear answers about where my money is. For a company handling rent payments, this level of support is unacceptable.” [...] Our summary is a good primer on this mess, but to really understand the chaos gripping the company and its customers, it helps to hear from users in their own words. We also compiled a sample of the one-star reviews posted on public websites about Bilt and shared these with the CFPB and with Congress. They are jaw-dropping.

> “Bilt extracted double rent payment from my account and as of today has not corrected their error. I have made multiple attempts to reach a live person with no success. I have sent a dispute letter. I have made multiple attempts to contact them with no success. My leasing office refuses to help me and identifies [B]ilt as a third party despite the requirement to process rent through [B]ilt.”
